preamble: The transformation from a voiced velar fricative [ɣ] to a voiced pharyngeal fricative [ʕ] is a process of pharyngealization, where the point of articulation moves backward into the pharynx.

examples.note: In certain contexts of vowel harmony or emphatic spread, velar fricatives can be retracted to pharyngeal positions.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Watson, J. C. (2002). The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic.
